AMBER › API Reference

API Reference

Complete reference for every public class, function, and constant in AMBER v2.1.0.

On this page
  1. Module-level helpers
  2. Map
  3. Classification
  4. TemporalMap
  5. TemporalAnalysis
  6. FeatureExtractor
  7. IterativeSOM
  8. Visualization
  9. Distances module

Module-level helpers

function vesanto_size(n)

Return the recommended SOM side length for n training samples using the Vesanto & Alhoniemi (2000) heuristic: max(2, round(√(5·√n))).

ParameterTypeDescription
nintNumber of training samples

Returns int — recommended side length.

constant AVAILABLE_DISTANCES

List of all supported distance metric names:
['euclidean', 'manhattan', 'chebyshev', 'cosine', 'correlation', 'dtw', 'cross_correlation']

Map

class AMBER.Map

Core Self-Organizing Map. Trains on a 2-D data array and exposes the trained weight grid.

Map.__init__(data, size=None, period=10, initial_lr=0.1, initial_neighbourhood=0, use_decay=False, distance='euclidean', normalization='none', weights='random', random_seed=None)
ParameterTypeDefaultDescription
datandarray (N, D)—Training data; rows = samples, columns = features
sizeint | NoneNoneMap side length. None = Vesanto auto-sizing (requires data)
periodint10Training epochs
initial_lrfloat0.1Starting learning rate (must be in (0, 1))
initial_neighbourhoodint0Starting neighbourhood radius; 0 = map_size
use_decayboolFalseIf True, Gaussian neighbourhood; if False, bubble function
distancestr'euclidean'BMU search distance. See AVAILABLE_DISTANCES
normalizationstr'none'Data normalisation strategy applied before each epoch
weightsstr'random'Weight initialisation: 'random', 'random_negative', 'sample', 'PCA'
random_seedint | NoneNoneSeed for the RNG. Pass an integer for fully reproducible training. Persisted in JSON save/load.
calculate_bmu(pattern) → tuple

Find the Best Matching Unit for a single pattern. Returns (distance, (row, col), weights, distance_matrix).

save_classifier(name: str)

Serialise the trained map to {name}.json.

Map.load_classifier(name: str) → Map classmethod

Load a previously saved map from {name}.json.

Key attributes

AttributeTypeDescription
map_sizeintSide length of the trained map
weights_ndarray (k, k, D)Trained weight grid

Classification

class AMBER.Classification

Assigns each sample in data to its BMU and computes map-quality metrics.

Classification.__init__(som, data, other=None, tagged=False, verbose: bool=False)

verbose=True logs debug info about labels and data via logging.debug.

Key attributes

AttributeTypeDescription
quantization_errorfloatMean distance (using the map's configured metric) between each sample and its BMU weight vector — primary quality metric
quantization_error_euclideanfloatMean Euclidean distance between samples and BMU weights, regardless of training metric — for cross-library / cross-paper comparison
distortionfloatGraepel–Heskes distortion measure: Gaussian-weighted mean squared distance of each sample to all neurons (vectorised)
topological_errorfloatFraction of samples whose 2nd-best BMU is non-adjacent to the 1st (Villmann 1997)
classification_mapDataFramePer-sample BMU position and distance; columns x, y, dist
activations_mapndarray (k, k)Count of BMU hits per neuron
umatrizndarray (2k-1, 2k-1)U-matrix of inter-neuron distances

TemporalMap

class AMBER.TemporalMap

Recurrent SOM (Voegtlin 2002). Extends Map with a context vector that accumulates sequence history. Accepts all Map parameters plus:

ParameterDefaultDescription
context_weight0.5α — memory decay rate (0 = no memory, 1 = full memory)
context_influence0.5β — fraction of distance contributed by context
random_seedNoneSeed for the RNG; passed through to Map.__init__. Persisted in JSON save/load.
reset_context()

Zero the context vector. Call between independent sequences.

TemporalAnalysis

class AMBER.TemporalAnalysis

Computes transition statistics from a Classification result.

Attribute / MethodDescription
trajectoryList of (row, col) BMU positions in sample order
transition_matrixndarray (k², k²) raw transition counts
transition_matrix_normndarray (k², k²) row-normalised transition probabilities
stabilityFraction of consecutive identical-BMU steps
mean_path_lengthMean Euclidean grid distance per step
mean_chebyshev_jumpMean Chebyshev (L∞) grid distance per step — counts diagonal steps as 1
temporal_coherenceFraction of consecutive steps with Chebyshev distance ≤ 1 (same neuron or immediate 8-neighbour). Range [0, 1]; standard SOM ≈ 0.4–0.6, well-tuned RSOM → 1.0
summary()Print all scalar metrics including TC and mean Chebyshev jump
dwell_times()Dict {(row,col): mean_dwell}
most_frequent_transitions(top_k)Sorted list of ((src, dst), count) pairs

FeatureExtractor

class AMBER.FeatureExtractor
FeatureExtractor.__init__(fs=None, n_mfcc=13)

fs — sampling frequency in Hz (required for spectral features). n_mfcc — number of MFCC coefficients.

extract(signal, features) → ndarray

Extract a 1-D feature vector from a 1-D signal window.

extract_batch(windows, features) → ndarray (N, F)

Vectorised extraction over a 2-D array of windows.

feature_names(features) → list[str]

Return feature labels in the same order as extract().

IterativeSOM

class AMBER.IterativeSOM

Trains maps for a range of sizes and selects the one with the lowest topological error.

best_map() → Map

Return the best Map found across all tested sizes.

calculate_range() → DataFrame

Return a DataFrame of size → QE / TE for all tested sizes.

Visualization

class AMBER.Visualization

All methods are static — call as Visualization.method(…).

MethodDescription
heat_map(cls, colorscale)Plotly interactive activation heat map
umatrix(cls, colorscale)Unified distance matrix (cluster boundaries)
umatrix_labeled(cls, labels, class_names, palette, …)U-matrix with coloured circle markers showing majority class and sample count per neuron. Legend placed above the axes.
hit_map(cls, labels, class_names, palette, …)Hit map where cell size scales with √(n/n_max) and background tint shows the majority class. Legend placed above the axes.
weight_map_grid(som, cls, labels, class_names, palette, …)k×k grid of subplots, one per neuron, each showing the weight vector as a line plot. Background tinted by majority class; dead neurons shown in grey.
elevation_map(cls)3-D surface of the U-matrix
characteristics_graph(som, row, col)Line plot of one neuron's weight vector
characteristics_bargraph(som, row, col, labels)Bar chart of one neuron's weight vector
codebook_vectors(som, headers)Heatmap of all weight vectors
trajectory(tcls, ta, background, …)Temporal trajectory overlaid on the map
transition_matrix_plot(ta, normalised)Heatmap of BMU transition probabilities
dwell_time_map(ta, tcls, …)Map coloured by mean dwell time per neuron

Example outputs

umatrix_labeled — U-matrix with class markers (Epilepsy dataset, 4 classes)

hit_map — Hit map with majority-class background tint (Epilepsy dataset)

weight_map_grid — Weight vector grid, one subplot per neuron (Epilepsy dataset)

Static map views

heat_map — BMU activation counts across the SOM grid (4×4 map, synthetic 3-class data)

umatrix — unified distance matrix; dark regions indicate cluster boundaries

elevation_map — 3-D surface of BMU activation counts

codebook_vectors — weight values per feature across all neurons

characteristics_graph — weight vector of neuron [0,0]

Temporal views

trajectory — time-ordered BMU sequence on the SOM grid (synthetic temporal data, 80 patterns)

transition_matrix_plot — row-normalised transition probabilities between neurons

dwell_time_map — mean number of consecutive steps spent on each neuron

Distances module

Import directly from AMBER.distances for standalone use:

from AMBER.distances import dtw_distance, cosine_distance_matrix

d = dtw_distance(signal_a, signal_b, band=20)
D = cosine_distance_matrix(weights, pattern)   # (rows, cols) array

Every signal distance has a scalar form foo_distance(a, b) and a matrix form foo_distance_matrix(W, p). Grid distances (grid_euclidean, grid_chebyshev) accept an ids_matrix and a bmu position.
